rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR (9th Line Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories And (2) Form Interrogatories, Moving Party Rudolph Koppl
rulings.Rulings: [Part 6 of 7 Motion to Enforce] Through this motion, Rudolph seeks an order to enforce the (1) Deposition Subpoena for the Production of Business Records directed to the Custodian of Records for DLD served on February 23, 2023; and (2) Deposition Subpoena for the Production of Business Records directed to the Custodian of Records for DML served on February 23, 2023 (the "Subpoenas"), over asserted consumer objections, as to Document Demand Nos. 1, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13 and 23. Per the declaration of attorney Jill Thorpe, a partner with the law firm of Delagnes, Linder & Duey, LLP and a partner with the accounting firm Delagnes, Mitchell & Linder, LLP, "[b]eginning in 2011, DLD and DML have and continue to maintain confidential client files on behalf of Frida Koppl, individually and as Settlor/Trustee of [the three Koppl trusts], and on behalf of Monica Zimmerman as current trustee of the three trusts." (Thorpe Decl. at par. 8). Rudolph has filed "Amended Petition to Interpret Trust". The Zimmerman's have filed "Petition to Determine Existence of Nonexistence Of Power, Duty, Or Right; To Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass Or Be Delivered; And To Pass Upon The Acts Of Frida Koppl, As Then Trustee." Both petitions are filed in connection with the Frida Koppl Trust One dated August 5, 2011 (the "Trust") and are pending before the court. Facially, the Document Demands at issue seek records related to Decedent's personal estate and tax planning efforts. Facially, they do not seek records pertaining to third parties, such as the Zimmermans individually or their business entities. The Document Demands at issue in this motion are relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending trust action. Rudolph has shown the subject of the motion will either be admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The court is persuaded by Rudolph's argument that the attorney-client privilege Decedent held in her individual capacity has not survived her death and the subsequent administration of her estate under her pour-over Will (see, HLC Prop, Ltd. v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 35 Cal4th 54; Moeller v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 1124, Evid. C sections 953, 954). Likewise, the court is persuaded by Rudolph's argument that Decedent's individual privacy rights did not survive her death (see, Hendrickson v. Cal. Newspapers (1975) 48 Cal.App.3d 59). The argument and case law cited in support of Respondents' Opposition indicates Respondents believe the Document Demands at issue encroach on privilege and/or privacy rights held by the current trustee of the Trust (see citation to Moeller v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 1124 and Wells Fargo Bank v. Sup. Ct. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 201). However, there is no showing that Monica Zimmerman has asserted an attorney-client privilege or privacy right specifically in her capacity as trustee. Her declaration in support of Respondents' Opposition states she has "never appeared in this action" and is not a party to this action in her in her capacity as trustee of the Trust (Monica Decl. at par. 6). The Zimmermans lodged and served their objections to the subpoenas in the capacity of non-party consumers. A motion to quash or modify the terms of the subpoena was not filed, by any party or non-party in any capacity (see Code of Civil. Proc. section 1987.1). [Part 6 of 7 Motion to Enforce]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR (10th Line Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories And (2) Form Interrogatories, Moving Party Rudolph Koppl
rulings.Rulings: [Part 7 of 7 Motion to Enforce] If a responding party objects to a discovery request based on a claim of privilege or a claim that the information sought is protected work product, the response must provide sufficient factual information for other parties to evaluate the merits of that claim, such as a privilege log. (CCP section 2031.240(c); Riddell, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 14 Cal App.5th 755, 772; Costco Wholesale Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2009), 47 Cal. 4th 725, 733 (party that seeks to protect communications from disclosure based upon the attorney-client privilege must establish the preliminary facts necessary to support its exercise)). A privilege log should provide a specific factual description of each document to which a claim of privilege is being made. (See Hernandez v. Sup. Ct. (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 285, 292.) Neither the Zimmermans nor DLD and DML have made a prima facia showing on this record to support the asserted privilege claims. There is no showing that a privilege log or other form of sufficient factual information in support of the claims of privilege has been produced in response to the Document Demands at issue. Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Enforce Compliance with Deposition Subpoenas for the Production of Business Records is GRANTED. The relevant custodian of records is ordered to respond to Deposition Subpoena for the Production of Business Records directed to the Custodian of Records for Delagnes, Linder & Duey, LLP, served on February 23, 2023, and Deposition Subpoena for the Production of Business Records directed to the Custodian of Records for Delagnes, Mitchell & Linder, LLP, served on February 23, 2023, as to Document Demand Nos. 1, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13 and 23, within 20 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1987.2(a). The court does not find that Respondents Monica and Alan Zimmerman acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Based on all of the evidence before the Court, Monica and Alan Zimmerman are ordered to pay Rudolph Koppl sanctions on this motion in the amount of forty-thousand dollars ($40,000), within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 7 of 7 Motion to Enforce]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories And (2) Form Interrogatories; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 4 Motion to Compel] Before the court is Petitioner and Respondent Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories and (2) Form interrogatories; and for Monetary Sanctions. Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman lodge objections to and seek to strike as inadmissible certain portions of the moving papers. The court rules on the evidentiary objections to each "specification," as termed by Respondents, as follows: SPECIFICATION No. 1, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:26-27. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 2, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:26-27. OBJECTION: Lack of supporting authority. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 3, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 7:13-14. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 4, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 10:19-23. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 5, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 11:21-23. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 6, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 11:24-25; 13:4-5; 14:8-9; 15:5-7; 16:3-4; 17:1-3; 18:5-6; 18:28-19:1; 19:26-28; 20:24-26; 21:23-25;22:23-24:1. OBJECTION: Lack of supporting authority. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 7, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 12:11-15] OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 8, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 12:3-4] OBJECTION: Hearsay, not subject to any exception. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 9, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 13:17-21. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 10, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 13:3-4. OBJECTION: Hearsay, not subject to any exception. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 11, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 17:11-16. OBJECTION: Hearsay, not subject to any exception. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. [Part 1 of 4 Motion to Compel]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR (2nd Line Added For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories And (2) Form Interrogatories, Moving Party Rudolph Koppl
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 4 Motion to Compel] Rudolph seeks an order compelling Respondent Monica Zimmerman ("Monica") to provide further responses to (1) Rudolph Koppl's Special Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("SI") Nos. 86 through 102, served on January 25, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2030.300 and (2) Rudolph Koppl's Form Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("FI") Nos. 1.1, 2.1, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.6, 14.1, and 17.1(b)-(d), served on January 25, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2030.300, on the grounds that the interrogatories seek information, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter of the action, and Monica's objections thereto are without merit or too general. The motion further seeks an award of monetary sanctions in the amount of $17,500 pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. sections 2023.030 and 2030.300(d). Monica's responses to the Special and Form Interrogatories at issue are entirely in the form of various objections, with no substantive responses. There is no showing that a privilege log or other form of sufficient factual information in support of the claims of privilege has been produced in response to the interrogatories at issue. If a responding party objects to a discovery request based on a claim of privilege or a claim that the information sought is protected work product, the response must provide sufficient factual information for other parties to evaluate the merits of that claim, such as a privilege log. (CCP section 2031.240(c); Riddell, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 14 Cal App.5th 755, 772; Costco Wholesale Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2009), 47 Cal. 4th 725, 733, party that seeks to protect communications from disclosure based upon the attorney-client privilege must establish the preliminary facts necessary to support its exercise). A privilege log should provide a specific factual description of each document to which a claim of privilege is being made. (See Hernandez v. Sup. Ct. (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 285, 292.) Monica has not made a prima facia showing on this record to support the asserted privilege claims. Any party to an action may obtain discovery regarding any matter not privileged that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action, if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, unless otherwise limited by court order in accordance with the Discovery Act. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010). Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery, or of any other party to the action. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010.) When a motion to compel discovery is brought, the burden generally rests upon the party seeking the discovery to provide evidence from which the court may determine the subject of the discovery motion "is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence." (Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 216, 223.) [Part 3 of 4 Motion to Compel]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR (3rd Line Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories And (2) Form Interrogatories, Moving Party Rudolph Koppl
rulings.Rulings: [Part 4 of 4 Motion to Compel] Rudolph has filed "Amended Petition to Interpret Trust." Monica and Alan Zimmerman have filed "Petition to Determine Existence of Nonexistence Of Power, Duty, Or Right; To Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass Or Be Delivered; And To Pass Upon The Acts Of Frida Koppl, As Then Trustee." Both petitions are filed in connection with the Frida Koppl Trust One dated August 5, 2011 (the "Trust"). At issue in both petitions is the validity, applicability, and effect of a document dated November 14, 2012, purportedly handwritten and signed by Decedent Frida Koppl, on the Trust and related testamentary instruments. Monica's Petition seeks a determination that "the 2012 Amendment was and is a valid amendment to [the Trust]" (11/27/18 Petition, 7:9-17). Rudolph's Amended Petition prays "for a ruling that the 2012 Handwriting was not a valid amendment to [the Trust]" (1/4/19 Amended Petition, 12:10-14). Rudolph Koppl's Special Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("SI") Nos. 86 through 102, served on January 25, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2030.300 and (2) Rudolph Koppl's Form Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("FI") Nos. 1.1, 2.1, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.6, 14.1, and 17.1(b)-(d), served on January 25, 2023, seek relevant, admissible evidence, or matter than appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, in the form of facts, documents, and identity of witnesses, concerning Monica's contentions regarding the validity, applicability, and effect of a handwritten document dated November 14, 2012, on the Trust and related testamentary instruments. The interrogatories at issue are relevant to the subject matter of the action. Petitioner and Respondent Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories and (2) Form interrogatories; and for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Further Responses shall be served within 10 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. Petitioner and Respondent Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. section 2030.300(d). The court does not find that Respondent Monica Zimmerman acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Based on all of the evidence before the Court, Monica Zimmerman is ordered to pay Rudolph Koppl sanctions on this motion in the amount of twenty-five thousand dollars ($25,000), within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 4 of 4 Motion to Compel]

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR (8th Line Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories And (2) Form Interrogatories, Moving Party Rudolph Koppl
rulings.Rulings: [Part 5 of 7 Motion to Enforce] SPECIFICATION No. 56, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:8-9. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, lack of foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 57, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:11-14. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, lack of foundation. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Improper Testimony Regarding Contents of a Writing. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, improper opinion. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 58, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:14-16. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Irrelevant and Prejudicial. Evid. Code sections 210, 350, 352. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 59, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:16-17. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, lack of foundation. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Improper Testimony Regarding Contents of a Writing. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, improper opinion. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 60, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:17-19. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Prejudicial. OVERRULED. The Zimmermans, in their capacities as non-parties whose personal consumer records are sought by the subpoena, served written objections on Rudolph, specifying grounds for prohibiting disclosure. Separately, the Zimmermans, in their capacity as Respondents in this action, have filed an Opposition to the Motion. The subpoenaed non-party entities DLD and DML have also filed an Opposition to the Motion. Any party to an action may obtain discovery regarding any matter not privileged that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action, if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, unless otherwise limited by court order in accordance with the Discovery Act. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010). Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery, or of any other party to the action. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010.) An attorney for any party may issue a business records deposition subpoena, which must be directed to the custodian of records or some other person qualified to authenticate the records. (Code of Civil Proc. sections 2020.210(b); 2020.410(c)). The requesting party may timely move for an order directing compliance with a deposition subpoena served on a nonparty. (Code of Civ. Proc. sections 1987.1(a), 2020.030). When a motion to compel discovery is brought, the burden generally rests upon the party seeking the discovery to provide evidence from which the court may determine the subject of the discovery motion "is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence." (Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 216, 223.) [Part 5 of 7 Motion to Enforce]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Rudolph Koppls Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For The Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 7 Motion to Enforce] Before the court is Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Enforce Compliance with Deposition Subpoenas for the Production of Business Records, pursuant to Code of Civil Proc. sections 1985.3(g), 1987.1, and 1987.2(a). Specifically, Rudolph seeks an order to enforce the (1) Deposition Subpoena for the Production of Business Records directed to the Custodian of Records for Delagnes, Linder & Duey, LLP ("DLD") served on February 23, 2023; and (2) Deposition Subpoena for the Production of Business Records directed to the Custodian of Records for Delagnes, Mitchell & Linder, LLP ("DML") served on February 23, 2023 (the "Subpoenas"), over asserted consumer objections, as to Document Demand Nos. 1, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13 and 23. Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman lodge objections to and seek to strike as inadmissible certain portions of (1) Andrew Zabronsky's declaration filed in support of the Motion to Enforce Compliance with Deposition Subpoenas for the Production of Business Records; (2) the Memorandum of Points and Authorities; and (3) the Separate Statement. The court rules on the evidentiary objections to each "specification," as termed by Respondents, as follows: SPECIFICATION No. 1 , Zabronsky Declaration, 6:3-5 OBJECTION: Improper Testimony Regarding Contents of a Writing. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, improper opinion. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 2, Zabronsky Declaration, 6:5-6. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Improper Testimony Regarding Contents of a Writing. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, lack of foundation. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Conclusory, improper opinion. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Improper argument. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 3, Zabronsky Declaration, 6:7-9. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 4, Zabronsky Declaration, 6:9-10. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Irrelevant and Prejudicial. Evid. Code sections 210, 350, 352. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 5, Zabronsky Declaration, 6:10-12. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 6, Zabronsky Declaration, 6:14. OBJECTION: Conclusory, lack of foundation. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Improper argument. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 7, Zabronsky Declaration, 6:14-15. OBJECTION: Improper argument. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 8, Zabronsky Declaration, 6:15-19. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation - Statement of Person. OVERRULED. [Part 1 of 7 Motion to Enforce]
